name: claude-multi-usage
description: Show 5h & 7d usage for multiple Claude accounts (auto-detected, freely labeled) in the status bar — plus per-account terminal launch.
author: southglory

commands.title: Claude Multi Usage: Open Usage Dashboard
Claude Multi Usage: Open Cache File

claudeMultiUsage.accounts.markdownDescription: Accounts to display (no count limit). `dir` is each account's CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (`~`/`%VAR%`/`${env}` expanded). **Leave empty** to auto-detect `.claude*` directories in your home folder. You can also add accounts via the `Claude: Add Account` command.

command.description: Command to run when opening this account. If empty, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is injected and launchCommand is run. If you set a cc-switch wrapper like `ccw`/`ccp`, that wrapper runs as-is (env injection is skipped — same as running ccw in a normal terminal).

claudeMultiUsage.warnAt.description: Turn yellow at or above this utilization (default 50%)

claudeMultiUsage.critAt.description: Turn red at or above this utilization

claudeMultiUsage.show7d.description: Also show the 7-day (weekly) % in the status bar label

claudeMultiUsage.fetchUsageViaApi.markdownDescription: When an account has no `vscode-claude-status-cache.json` (the Claude Code VS Code integration only writes it for the one account it polls), fetch that account's usage directly from the Anthropic API using its `.credentials.json` OAuth token and the rate-limit response headers — the same method `long-kudo.vscode-claude-status` uses. This is the only way to show usage for a second account used only in a terminal. Sends a tiny ~1-token request (≈$0.0001); disable to use cache files only.

claudeMultiUsage.apiMinIntervalSeconds.markdownDescription: Minimum seconds between API usage fetches per account (default 5 min). After the first reading, the API is only called again when that account was actually used within this window — so when you stop using Claude Code, the calls stop. Only applies when `fetchUsageViaApi` is on.

claudeMultiUsage.showCharacter.description: Show the breathing quokka mascot before each account

claudeMultiUsage.enableAnimation.description: Animate the mascot's up/down breathing (requires showCharacter)

claudeMultiUsage.animationPeriodMs.description: One full breathing cycle (ms), divided by the number of frames

claudeMultiUsage.characterFrames.description: Mascot animation frames (defaults to the 2-frame pixel quokka up/down breathing). Replace with any text/codicon/emoji you like (e.g. ["▃","▆"]).

claudeMultiUsage.pricing.inputPerMillion.description: Cost estimate: USD per 1M input tokens (default = Sonnet API price)

claudeMultiUsage.pricing.outputPerMillio.description: Cost estimate: USD per 1M output tokens

claudeMultiUsage.pricing.cacheReadPerMil.description: Cost estimate: USD per 1M cache-read tokens

claudeMultiUsage.pricing.cacheCreatePerM.description: Cost estimate: USD per 1M cache-creation tokens

claudeMultiUsage.dailyBudget.description: Optional daily spend budget (USD). 0 = off. When set, the dashboard shows how much of today's budget is used.

claudeMultiUsage.launchCommand.description: Command to run when opening an account terminal. If empty, only the environment variable is set and nothing is run.

claudeMultiUsage.clickAction.description: Action when an item is left-clicked. Dashboard / Terminal / Cache / Settings are also reachable via the hover tooltip links (VS Code does not support a custom right-click menu on status bar items).
